preamble: The shift from a retroflex stop [ʈ] to the alveolar nasal [n] involves two phonetic changes: nasalization and de-retroflexion. This shift typically occurs through an intermediate retroflex nasal [ɳ] stage, which then fronts to the standard alveolar position as a language simplifies its phonology or neutralizes retroflex contrasts.

examples.note: Many modern South Asian languages have neutralized the historical contrast between retroflex and dental nasals, merging them into the standard alveolar [n].
Suffix allomorphy shows the fluid boundary between retroflex stops and nasals in rapid speech.
